etymology.description: 聲(声) (shēng, "stone chime") + 殳(殳) (shū, "hand holding a hammer or a mallet") = a stone chime being struck by a mallet -> vessel; sound
etymology.interpretation: The character 殸(kēng, "vessel; sound") visually represents a hand wielding a mallet striking a stone chime, signifying the action of creating sound or the vessel on which it is made. This composition intentionally evokes the sounds produced by striking.

parts.english: hand holding a hammer or a mallet
